adjustments.reason: Brazil converted first-half pressure into a 3-0 lead after a disappointing opening draw.
Cunha scored twice and provides a clearer central finishing signal when Neymar is absent.
Brazil won 3-0 but still showed slow possession, offside-heavy direct balls and limited fluidity.
After a large first-half lead, Brazil conserved energy rather than chasing a larger score.
Raphinha left with a first-half injury; this matters for the Scotland match.
AP reported Ancelotti expects Neymar back against Scotland, but return timing and minutes remain uncertain.
Saibari scored after roughly 70 seconds against Scotland.
Morocco protected a one-goal lead and appear close to qualification.
Scotland improved after the early setback and created late pressure.
Scotland had physical pressure and crosses but lacked consistent high-quality open-play chances.
Haiti have not scored in two games and struggled to create sustained threat against Brazil.
The McTominay non-penalty controversy shows contact-box variance can materially affect low-scoring games.
